channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: What’s a Threat? Gabbard Says It’s Up to Trump, on Iran and Elsewhere.
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, was left to square the president’s comments about an imminent nuclear threat from Iran with a letter from one of her trusted aides.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, during a Senate hearing into worldwide threats on Wednesday. She said only the president can decide what constitutes an imminent threat.

item.title: The Iran War’s Economic Threat to Europe and Asia
item.description: Inflation and its consequences for growth are a growing concern for countries where memories of the 2022 energy crisis are fresh.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson and River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Mohammad Ponir Hossain/Reuters
item.media:description: A fuel station in Dhaka, Bangladesh, last week.

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Saw No Change in Iran’s Missile Capabilities Before War
item.description: On Wednesday, the director of national intelligence and C.I.A. director contradicted one of the justifications the Trump administration had given for its attacks on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Tulsi Gabbard, the director of national intelligence, testified before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Wednesday.

item.title: Mullin Signals He Would End Noem Policy That Slowed Disaster Aid
item.description: The president’s nominee, Markwayne Mullin, said he would avoid “micromanaging” FEMA.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Markwayne Mullin, Republican of Oklahoma, at his confirmation hearing for Department of Homeland Security secretary on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump’s Planning for America’s 250th Emphasizes Religion’s Role in the Nation’s Founding
item.description: A closed-door White House event included news about the National Garden of American Heroes and an emphasis on the role of religion in the founding.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Schuessler and Zachary Small

item.title: Bondi Doesn’t Commit to Deposition With House Panel Over Epstein Files
item.description: Under the rules of the oversight committee, Attorney General Pam Bondi received a subpoena requiring her to appear. The panel’s Republican chairman said he sent the summons reluctantly.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Attorney General Pam Bondi after a closed-door briefing for members of the House Oversight Committee on Wednesday.

item.title: Family of Minneapolis Boy Detained by ICE Faces Fast-Track Deportation
item.description: The family of 5-year-old Liam Conejos Ramos, who became a symbol of Trump’s immigration crackdown, is appealing their accelerated removal.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: A photo of Liam Conejo Ramos was displayed during a Congressional hearing in Washington n February.

item.title: Cesar Chavez, a Civil Rights Icon, Is Accused of Abusing Girls for Years
item.description: An investigation by The New York Times found extensive evidence that the United Farm Workers co-founder groomed and sexually abused girls who worked in the movement.
item.dc:creator: Manny Fernandez and Sarah Hurtes
item.media:credit: John Sotomayor/The New York Times
item.media:description: Cesar Chavez during a demonstration in New York in 1969.

item.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady as War in Iran Upends the Economic Outlook
item.description: Jerome H. Powell, the chair of the Federal Reserve, emphasized the high degree of uncertainty stemming from the conflict as he acknowledged the potential for surging energy prices to lift inflation and dent growth.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair. “The thing I really want to emphasize is that nobody knows,” he said on Wednesday regarding the war in Iran’s effects on the economy.

item.title: Powell Says He Will Remain as Fed Chair Until Successor Is Confirmed
item.description: Jerome H. Powell, who leads the central bank, also said he would not leave the Fed until a criminal investigation into his handling of renovations was over.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.media:credit: Brendan Mcdermid/Reuters
item.media:description: Jerome H. Powell, the Federal Reserve chair, can stay on as chair of the central bank until a successor is named, and can remain as a governor until 2028.

item.title: The Cost of the A.I. Boom: A Trade Deficit the President Detests
item.description: A recent surge of A.I.-related imports has become an impediment to the smaller trade deficit President Trump wants.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Unlike cars, steel and other goods, President Trump has intentionally avoided putting import taxes on electronics.

item.title: Ozempic Is About to Go Generic in India, China and Canada
item.description: In India, China and several other nations, Novo Nordisk is on the verge of losing patent protection for its blockbuster weight loss drug, opening the door for cheaper competing versions.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Robbins, Meaghan Tobin, Eshe Nelson, Alex Travelli and Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Saumya Khandelwal for The New York Times

item.title: Following Trump, Republicans in Congress Propose to Ban Most Voting by Mail
item.description: A restrictive voter I.D. bill under consideration in the Senate could severely limit mail-in voting. Conservatives are pressing to end the practice outright, taking aim at an option that is widely used by voters.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Rachel Wisniewski for The New York Times
item.media:description: Democrats are solidly opposed to the measure, and even some Republicans are balking at the added restrictions on voting by mail.

item.title: Stratton, Illinois Senate Primary Winner, Promises a ‘Fight’ With Trump
item.description: Ms. Stratton, a Democrat whose viral campaign ad featured voters’ profane views of Mr. Trump, said her campaign’s aggressive messaging was resonating.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Joshua Lott for The New York Times
item.media:description: Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton of Illinois during a campaign event last week. She will enter the general election as the prohibitive favorite in her deep-blue state.

item.title: Palestinian Man Recounts Brutal Sexual Assault by Israeli Settlers
item.description: The man said his attackers stripped him naked, beat him and zip-tied his genitalia, an account corroborated by family members and a rights activist who were also beaten.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.media:credit: Afif Amireh for The New York Times
item.media:description: Suhaib Abualkebash, with a bruised eye, in Khirbet Humsa, in the Israeli-occupied West Bank. He said he had been assaulted by Israeli settlers.

item.title: In Swaths of Germany, the Far-Right AfD Is Part of the Fabric
item.description: The Alternative for Germany party, or AfD, is shunned in federal politics but is a regional force. This fall, it could win broader power for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley, Tatiana Firsova and Clay Risen
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
item.media:description: Three AfD representatives — Christian Skotnik, left, Doreen Gärtner, center, and Enrico Schult — before a party meeting in Stavenhagen, Germany, in October.

item.title: Silicon Valley Musters Behind-the-Scenes Support for Anthropic
item.description: Tech companies have been reluctant to directly confront Trump administration officials over their contract feud with the A.I. start-up.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Anthropic’s stance on how its artificial intelligence can be used in autonomous weapons and domestic surveillance infuriated officials at the Pentagon.

item.title: Google Sits Pretty as A.I. Rivals Compete for Pentagon Favor
item.description: The tech giant has been rebuilding its relationship with the Defense Department and is poised to benefit as it sidesteps competitors’ controversies.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Google, which swore off military work in 2018, is quietly rebuilding its relationship with the Defense Department.

item.title: The Tropical St. Patrick’s Day That Honors African History
item.description: Montserrat treats the holiday as both a national celebration and a more somber milestone: a commemoration of a failed slave rebellion.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner and Luis Antonio Rojas

item.title: Carol Kitman, 96, Dies; Photographer Documented the Vindman Twins
item.description: A chance encounter in Brooklyn led to a decades-long project following the boys’ lives, from childhood to national prominence as critics of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: Trump-Ukraine Whistle-Blower Complaint and Impeachment Inquiry

item.title: How Trump Should Extricate Himself From His Iran Quagmire
item.description: Declaring victory and ending the war might not be so easy.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kristof
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Kim Hoeckele for The New York Times

item.title: Joe Kent’s Resignation Letter Is Dangerous Because It’s Half True
item.description: Kent’s resignation letter is partly rooted in truth, even if it taps into old antisemitic tropes about occult Jewish control.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Goldberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Mattie Neretin/Reuters

item.title: Late Night Tells the Tale of Dueling Senators
item.description: “Also very funny to describe a duel as being between two consenting adults. You know, because if only one person consents, that’s murder,” said Seth Meyers, host of “Late Night.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Joseph Duggar of ’19 Kids and Counting’ Faces Child Sex Abuse Charges
item.description: Mr. Duggar, a former star of the TLC reality show, was arrested in Arkansas and was awaiting extradition to Florida, where the authorities said he molested a 9-year-old girl in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.media:credit: Ida Mae Astute/Disney General Entertainment Content via Getty Images
item.media:description: Joseph Duggar in 2016. Mr. Duggar, a former star of the TLC series “19 Kids and Counting,” was arrested in Arkansas on Wednesday, the authorities said.

item.title: NASA’s Hubble Telescope Spots Comet K1 Exploding Into Fragments
item.description: In a stroke of luck, astronomers saw the comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) break into four or five fragments in November after it passed close to the sun.
item.dc:creator: Robin George Andrews
item.media:credit: NASA, ESA, Dennis Bodewits (AU); Image Processing: Joseph DePasquale (STScI)

item.title: He Tried to Rob 6 Banks, Police Say. His Total Takings: $605.
item.description: A man is accused of handing tellers notes, demanding money at six Chase branches in five days. In three of the attempts, he left empty-handed.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman
item.media:credit: John Taggart for The New York Times
item.media:description: A man is accused of robbing or attempting to rob several Chase locations in New York City.
